Categories.value: Roxburgh Building, Shibpur|GFDL|Taken with Nikon D3200|License migration redundant|Self-published work|FoP-India|Photographs by Biswarup Ganguly|March 2013 India photographs

ImageDescription.value: The Roxburgh Building, 200 year old structure of the botanical garden at Howrah, was known as ‘East India Company’s Garden’ or the ‘Company Bagan’ or ‘Calcutta Garden’ and later as the ‘Royal Botanic Garden’ which after independence was renamed as the ‘Indian Botanic Garden’ in 1950. It came under the management of the Botanical Survey of India on January 1, 1963. From June 25, 2009 it is renamed as ‘Acharya Jagadish Chandra Bose Indian Botanic Garden’.
